TEXEL FORGE · PROTOTYPES/PLATFORMS

Platform Kit Builder — run report

build_kit.py <scene> <prefix> — one command, no hand boxes: crisp downscale (skipped when input is already app-native) → rim discovery → band detection (staircases rejected, wall-backed slabs cropped below the divider) → tileability scoring → donor-local cuts → terminus classification + SAM end harvest (rim-flush gated) → demo strips. Donors with genuine ends lead the ranking.

Harvested end tiles (SAM silhouette alpha, 8×; judge these)

Demo strips (synthesized ~2.5× donor width; pixel-crisp, scroll sideways)

GENERATIVE END CAPS (NEW)

SAM tile harvest proved the weak stage, so ends are now generated: the procedural strip is composed over a known sky canvas and gpt-image-2 paints each end via a per-side context crop; silhouettes come from a pixel diff against the known background — no SAM. Mask band height and one prompt clause control the style (decorated corbels vs flush).

READING THE RESULTS

  • Tileability = median ÷ best cut-pair cost inside the donor. Higher means the texture has genuinely repeatable mortar lines; ~2.0 is the acceptance floor.
  • Ends (support-map v3): each donor's interior defines its material as a set of quantized palette colors; a per-column support curve (palette membership × cap-rim signature) measures where the platform actually extends. Termini come from where support drops and stays dropped — short dips (props, shadows) are bridged. Each side reports end with a confidence, border (runs off-image), or ? (unknown — connects to structures, or ambiguous; deliberately no guess). SAM only cuts silhouettes at confirmed termini; "no tile" means the silhouette failed quality gates and would go to user confirmation in the product.
  • Status: terminus false positives are gone (mask boundaries no longer count as ends). Silhouette harvest is the weak stage — roughly half of confirmed termini fail the mask gates. Distinctive-feature repetition (drips, drapery) remains the small-donor problem, pending cross-platform pooling.
